summary: F5 step 3-I add_turn envelope variant test PARTIAL. All wire-level fixes from prior iterations remain INTACT (close code 1000, no SyntaxError, no 'Close received after close'). However add_turn envelope NOT rendered as chat bubble — same silent-drop end-state as #671's neo_error. NO 'Unknown command add_turn' log either. CAVEAT: this test run had unusually heavy network errors (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET to multiple sub-endpoints, ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR, ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR, 'Failed to authenticate websocket' messages) — engine inject may have fired against disposed socket due to auth cycling. Recommend ONE re-run before locking in PARTIAL. If PARTIAL confirmed, try envelope variants (update_turn / turn_state / add_chat_message). Mission protection HOLDS.

key_findings.1_all_wire_level_layers_still_green: Close code 1000, no SyntaxError, no 'Close received' — all 3 cumulative wire-level fixes (#666 + #669 + #671) still working perfectly. add_turn change is purely envelope schema layer.
key_findings.2_add_turn_silent_drop: Same drop-without-warning behavior as neo_error in #671. character.ai onmessage dispatcher ignores add_turn command silently. No 'Unknown command' log generated either way — diagnostic is harder than expected.
key_findings.3_flaky_network_caveat: This run had heavy network errors (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET cluster + auth cycling). Engine inject may not have actually delivered to a healthy socket. Re-run recommended before locking PARTIAL.
key_findings.4_mission_protection_unbroken_streak_7: Mission state HELD across 7 iterations (#666 → #672). Engine block is rock solid; only UI delivery iterations changing.

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: Wire-level inject mechanism is COMPLETE and stable. add_turn envelope was the right ARCHITECTURAL approach (mimic legitimate server response shape) but the specific schema variant doesn't match what character.ai's onmessage dispatcher accepts.
next_steps: FIRST: re-run this test (#672 retry) when network is stable — current run had ERR_CONNECTION_RESET cluster that may have prevented inject from reaching healthy socket
If PARTIAL confirmed on re-run, try envelope variants in priority order:
1. command:'update_turn' (incremental update path — most likely accepted mid-stream)
3. command:'add_chat_message' (explicit message-addition verb in some neo APIs)
4. Author shape variant: {is_human:false, name:'Security Filter', author_id:'system'}
Optimal alternative: HAR capture during a successful non-PII chat to identify EXACT server turn response schema, then mirror it precisely
Continue current build in production — mission protection working perfectly across all iterations
